title: Contraception review with migraine history and thrombotic risk

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: You wanted to restart the combined pill. Any migraines, aura, blood clots, smoking, or high blood pressure?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I get migraines with zig-zag lights before the headache. I do not smoke. My mum had a clot after surgery.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any personal clot, stroke, chest pain, or current pregnancy possibility?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No personal clot or stroke. Pregnancy test yesterday was negative.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Migraine with aura means the combined pill is not safe because of stroke risk. We can use a progestogen-only pill, implant, IUD, or injection.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I did not realise. I might try the progestogen-only pill.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: We will start that, discuss missed-pill rules, and review bleeding or side effects in three months. Seek urgent care for chest pain, leg swelling, severe new headache, weakness, or vision loss.

candidate_notes.note: Contraception review. Patient requested combined pill but has migraine with aura (zig-zag lights before headache). Non-smoker. No personal clot/stroke; pregnancy test negative. Family clot after surgery. Combined hormonal contraception contraindicated due to aura/stroke risk. Options discussed; patient prefers progestogen-only pill. Missed-pill rules discussed. Review 3 months. Urgent advice for chest pain, leg swelling, severe new headache, weakness, vision loss.
Contraceptive pill review. No smoking or personal clot history. Combined oral contraceptive pill restarted. Discussed side effects and review in three months.

S: Wants contraception. Migraine with visual aura. Non-smoker, no personal clot/stroke, negative pregnancy test. A: Combined pill contraindicated. P: Discussed progestogen-only pill/implant/IUD/injection; start progestogen-only pill and review 3 months.

Migraine with aura identified, so combined hormonal contraception avoided due to stroke risk. No personal clot/stroke, non-smoker, pregnancy test negative. Progestogen-only options discussed; patient chooses POP. Missed-pill rules and side effects reviewed. Urgent advice: chest pain, leg swelling, severe new headache, weakness, vision loss. Review 3 months.

CDSS contraception safety check: migraine with aura is contraindication to combined hormonal contraception. Combined pill not prescribed. Progestogen-only methods offered; POP started after negative pregnancy test and no personal VTE/stroke. Missed-pill counselling, 3-month review, and thrombotic/neuro red flags documented.
